hero.headline: Terms of Service
hero.subheadline: Clear, fair terms for our Square API integration services
hero.plain_english_summary: We build Square API integrations. You pay us. We deliver great work. Everyone's happy. The details below make it official.

content.parties: These terms form a legal agreement between Squared² Pty Ltd (ABN 12 345 678 901) ('we', 'us', 'our') and you or the entity you represent ('you', 'your', 'client').
content.acceptance: By engaging our services, accessing our tools, or signing a proposal, you agree to these terms. If you're agreeing for a company, you promise you have authority to do so.
content.precedence: Specific project agreements override these general terms where they conflict.

what_we_do: Square API consulting and advisory services

how_we_work.discovery: We'll understand your needs and propose a solution
how_we_work.agreement: You approve the proposal and we agree on timeline/budget
how_we_work.development: We build your integration with regular updates
how_we_work.delivery: We deploy, test, and train your team

content.not_included: Unless specifically agreed, we don't provide: general IT support, Square account setup, payment processing, or non-API related services.

content.intro: Project success requires collaboration. You agree to:

responsibilities.detail: Tell us what you need clearly and completely
Provide Square API credentials and system access as needed
Answer questions and approve work within agreed timeframes
Review deliverables and report issues promptly
Honor payment terms outlined in your agreement

content.delays: Delays in your responsibilities may impact timeline and costs.

content.your_data: You retain all rights to your business data, content, and pre-existing intellectual property.

custom_code.ownership: Custom code we create specifically for you becomes your property upon final payment.
custom_code.license_to_us: You grant us a license to showcase the project (anonymized) in our portfolio.

our_tools.ownership: We retain rights to our pre-existing tools, frameworks, and methodologies.
our_tools.license_to_you: You receive a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use them in your integration.

content.square_property: Square owns their APIs and documentation. We don't claim any rights to Square's intellectual property.

content.structure: Payment terms are specified in your project agreement. Typically:
standard_terms: Fixed price projects: 40% upfront, 40% at milestone, 20% on completion
Hourly consulting: Monthly invoicing, NET 14 days
Support contracts: Monthly or quarterly in advance
content.invoicing: We'll send tax invoices with ABN and GST clearly shown.

late_payment.grace_period: 7 days grace period on all invoices
late_payment.suspension: We may suspend work for payments 30+ days overdue

content.disputes: Dispute specific line items, not entire invoices. Undisputed amounts must be paid on time.

content.mutual_nda: We both agree to keep confidential information private:
our_obligations: Keep your business information strictly confidential
Only use your data for project purposes
Limit access to team members who need it
Return or delete your data after project completion
your_obligations: Keep our methodologies and pricing confidential
Not recruit our team members during and 12 months after project
Respect confidentiality of our other clients
content.exceptions: Confidentiality doesn't apply to information that's public, independently developed, or legally required to be disclosed.
content.duration: Confidentiality obligations survive for 5 years after project completion.

what_we_warrant: Professional service delivery with reasonable skill and care
Integration will substantially conform to agreed specifications
We have the right to provide the services
content.warranty_period: 30 days from delivery for defect correction at no charge
what_we_dont_warrant: Integration will be error-free or uninterrupted
Specific business outcomes (unless guaranteed in writing)
content.remedy: For warranty breaches, we'll fix the issue or refund the affected portion.

content.cap: Our total liability is limited to the amount you paid for the affected services in the 12 months before the claim.
content.exclusions: Neither party is liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages, including lost profits, even if warned of the possibility.
content.exceptions: Limitations don't apply to breaches of confidentiality, willful misconduct, or indemnification obligations.
content.insurance: We maintain professional indemnity insurance of $2 million.

content.by_us: We'll defend you against claims that our deliverables infringe third-party intellectual property rights.
content.by_you: You'll defend us against claims arising from your use of the integration, your data, or breach of these terms.
content.process: The indemnified party must promptly notify, reasonably cooperate, and let the indemnifying party control defense.

content.completion: Agreement ends naturally when we deliver and you pay.
content.for_convenience: Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice.
content.for_cause: Either party may terminate immediately for material breach not cured within 14 days of notice.

effect.payment: You pay for work completed to termination date
effect.delivery: We provide work product in current state
effect.survival: Confidentiality, IP, and limitation clauses survive

content.events: Neither party is liable for delays due to events beyond reasonable control (natural disasters, war, pandemic restrictions, Square API outages).
content.process: Affected party must promptly notify and mitigate impact.
content.extended_delay: If force majeure exceeds 30 days, either party may terminate without penalty.

content.governing_law: Victorian law governs these terms. Courts of Victoria have exclusive jurisdiction.
content.entire_agreement: These terms plus your project agreement are the complete agreement. Previous discussions don't count unless written here.
content.changes: Amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties.
content.assignment: You can't assign rights without our written consent. We may assign to a successor.
content.severability: If any term is invalid, the rest remain in effect.
content.waiver: Not enforcing a right doesn't waive it for the future.
content.notices: Official notices must be in writing to the addresses in your project agreement.
content.relationship: We're independent contractors, not partners or employees.

content.approach: We believe in solving problems through communication:

process.description: First, we talk directly to resolve issues
Senior management from both sides meet

content.costs: Each party bears own costs until mediation (split 50/50).

terms: Free to use for testing and exploration
Don't use for production or real transactions
We're not liable for playground availability
Usage data may inform our service development
Based on typical results from similar businesses
Response times are during business hours
Excluded: training, new features, third-party issues
Annual contracts include quarterly health checks

questions.headline: Questions About These Terms?
questions.text: We believe in clear communication. If anything's unclear, ask us.

plain_english.commitment: We've written these terms as clearly as possible while maintaining legal effectiveness. We want you to understand what you're agreeing to.
plain_english.summary: The short version: We'll do great work. You'll pay us fairly. We'll both act professionally and protect each other's interests. If problems arise, we'll work them out like adults.
